claim_restated: Config bootstrap is non-transactional and order-dependent: (1) read path triggers migration when config.json is missing via load_config -> _load_config_payload -> _migrate_from_state_files (config.py:136-144, 177-184), (2) migration source order is non-deterministic (glob) with first-writer scalar precedence (config.py:396-401, 322-336), (3) source state files are destructively rewritten (del state['config']) before config.json is durably persisted (config.py:357-363, 371-381), (4) config.json write failure is best-effort logged without rollback (config.py:403-409).
why_poor_engineering: The claims are technically accurate — a read-path operation (load_config) triggers destructive migration side effects, glob ordering is non-deterministic, source files are stripped before destination persistence, and write failure is silently swallowed. This violates CQS and creates a narrow but real data-loss window.

why_missed.what_should_catch: A round-trip migration test that fails config.json write after state files are stripped, verifying config keys are not lost.
why_missed.why_not_caught: The migration path only runs on first use (no config.json exists yet) and the failure window requires an OSError during config write while state writes succeed — same directory, same function, unlikely in practice.
why_missed.what_could_catch: A fault-injection test that mocks save_config to raise OSError after _strip_config_from_state_file has run, then asserts config recovery on the next load.

claims.title: Read path triggers migration when config.json is missing
claims.detail: config.py:136-144: _load_config_payload calls _migrate_from_state_files when config file does not exist. Already verified in S313 verdict (Claim 1).

claims.title: Migration source enumeration is unsorted (glob) with first-writer scalar precedence
claims.detail: config.py:396-401: state_dir.glob('state-*.json') returns filesystem order. config.py:322-336: _merge_config_value uses first-writer semantics. Already verified in S313 verdict (Claim 2).

claims.title: Source state files are destructively rewritten before config.json persistence
claims.detail: config.py:357-368: _strip_config_from_state_file deletes state_data['config'] and rewrites the file before save_config is called at config.py:405. Already verified in S313 verdict (Claim 3).

claims.title: Config.json write failure is best-effort only
claims.detail: config.py:403-409: save_config is wrapped in try/except OSError with log_best_effort_failure. Already verified in S313 verdict (Claim 4).

fix_description: No fix needed — verdict is NO due to zero originality. All claims were already verified in S313.
notes: DUPLICATE — zero originality. All 4 claims in S003 are identical to claims already verified in S313 (bounty-verdicts/S313-juzigu40-ui-s02-supplemental.md) from the same author @juzigu40-ui. S313 explicitly identifies itself as a 'supplemental argument that S02 (config bootstrap non-transactional migration)' — same finding, same code references, same reasoning. The technical claims are accurate but have already been credited. Scores are 0/10 across all dimensions because no new information is contributed.
